beat: every 2024-vintage 'autonomous AI workforce' company is quietly pivoting to 'human-in-the-loop' and pretending it was always the plan
a coding-IDE company just raised at a valuation higher than most nation-states' GDP while every smaller code-gen startup quietly puts itself up for sale
a Chinese open-weights model benchmarks above the frontier labs again and the SF Slack channels have not stopped melting down since
late-stage unicorns are doing 'structured secondaries' that are mathematically down-rounds but PR'd as up-rounds, and the press is finally calling it out
the H1B premium-pricing era is here; founders openly ask VCs for 'visa bridge' carve-outs and half the YC batch incorporates a Canadian sub as a hedge
the YC batch is back over 400 companies, with at least 30 pitching some flavor of 'AI for [legacy industry] compliance' and three of them having identical landing pages
every consumer-AI founder who couldn't raise a Series A in Q1 has suddenly discovered a passion for autonomous drones and DoD contracts
the OpenAI / Anthropic / xAI / Meta four-way poaching circus where engineers get term sheets like free agents and Twitter keeps a public leaderboard
humanoid-robot companies are raising $500M Series Bs on demo videos that are 80% post-production, and everyone in the room knows it
two AI infra companies just filed S-1s within a week of each other and the bankers are pretending the 2021 SPAC boom never happened
